Operation 'LegalNotice' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is too brief and lacks context about the response format (gzip-compressed HTML), file purpose, and authentication requirements. Improved summary uses action-oriented language. Enhanced description clarifies what the operation returns and its format, helping AI agents understand when to use this endpoint for license/attribution information.

data.current_description: All licenses of programs used by Home.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the compressed legal notice file containing all licenses and attributions for programs and libraries used by the Google Home device. Returns a gzip-compressed HTML document (NOTICE.html.gz) containing license information. This is a static file endpoint requiring no authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks context about the response format (gzip-compressed HTML), file purpose, and authentication requirements. Improved summary uses action-oriented language. Enhanced description clarifies what the operation returns and its format, helping AI agents understand when to use this endpoint for license/attribution information.

message: Operation 'ChromecastIcon' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, unclear_purpose, missing_output_details. Original description is confusing with contradictory information ("no longer exists" vs "redirect to..."). Improved version clarifies deprecation status, explains historical purpose, provides the redirect URL for reference, and indicates expected HTTP response codes. This helps AI agents understand the endpoint is deprecated and should not be relied upon.

data.current_description:
**Update:** This no longer exists. It's not useful, anyway.

 A redirect to `http://www.gstatic.com/eureka/images/eureka_device.png`
data.description_suggestion: This endpoint is deprecated and no longer functional on the device. Historically, it provided a redirect to the Chromecast device icon image hosted at Google's static content server (http://www.gstatic.com/eureka/images/eureka_device.png). This endpoint should not be used in new implementations. Returns HTTP 301/302 redirect or 404 Not Found.
data.reasoning: Original description is confusing with contradictory information ("no longer exists" vs "redirect to..."). Improved version clarifies deprecation status, explains historical purpose, provides the redirect URL for reference, and indicates expected HTTP response codes. This helps AI agents understand the endpoint is deprecated and should not be relied upon.

message: Operation 'Scanfordevices' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about timeout parameter, caching behavior, and authentication requirements. Added specificity about what devices are discovered, how results are accessed, and authentication needs for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
This initiates scan for other bluetooth speakers/devices. Scan results will be updated continuously for `timeout` seconds.
To get the scan results, see /setup/bluetooth/scan_results.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a Bluetooth scan to discover nearby speakers and devices. The scan runs for the specified timeout duration (in seconds) and continuously updates results. Scan results are cached and can be retrieved via GET /setup/bluetooth/scan_results. Requires local authorization token.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about timeout parameter, caching behavior, and authentication requirements. Added specificity about what devices are discovered, how results are accessed, and authentication needs for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'GetPairedDevices' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is clear and actionable. Description is vague about what information is returned and lacks authentication context. Enhanced description specifies return data and authentication requirement for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: This gives a list of all paired or 'bonded' devices. The response field names are self-descriptive.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all Bluetooth paired (bonded) devices. Returns device information including device name, address, and connection status. Requires local authorization token.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description is vague about what information is returned and lacks authentication context. Enhanced description specifies return data and authentication requirement for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'ConnecttoWi-FiNetwork' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Summary is clear but description is unhelpful for AI agents. Converted informal note into structured description explaining the operation's intent, requirements, current limitations, and authentication needs. This helps agents understand the operation exists but is not yet usable.

data.current_description: **Note:** Not sure how the password is encrypted. Might be using the public certificate from /setup/eureka_info. So this cannot be used as of now. If someone figures it out, please [create a new issue here](https://github.com/rithvikvibhu/GHLocalApi/issues/new).
data.description_suggestion: Connects the device to a specified Wi-Fi network. Requires SSID and password. Password encryption method is currently undocumented - may use public certificate from /setup/eureka_info. This endpoint is not currently functional pending clarification of password encryption. Requires local authorization token.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is unhelpful for AI agents. Converted informal note into structured description explaining the operation's intent, requirements, current limitations, and authentication needs. This helps agents understand the operation exists but is not yet usable.

message: Operation 'GetSavedNetworks' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is clear. Description contains TODO comment and lacks context about wpa_auth/wpa_cipher values. Restructured to explain field purposes, clarify wpa_id usage for other operations, and add authentication requirement. Removed TODO comment for cleaner AI agent interpretation.

data.current_description:
This gets a list of all saved Wi-Fi networks.

Each network has `ssid`, `wpa_auth`, `wpa_cipher` and `wpa_id`.
`wpa_id` is an incrementing number used to identify a saved network.
#TODO: Add values for `wpa_auth` and `wpa_cipher`.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all saved Wi-Fi networks configured on the device. Each network entry includes: ssid (network name), wpa_id (unique identifier for the network), wpa_auth (authentication type), and wpa_cipher (encryption cipher). Use wpa_id to reference networks in other operations like forget_wifi. Requires local authorization token.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear. Description contains TODO comment and lacks context about wpa_auth/wpa_cipher values. Restructured to explain field purposes, clarify wpa_id usage for other operations, and add authentication requirement. Removed TODO comment for cleaner AI agent interpretation.

message: Operation 'ForgetWi-FiNetwork' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear. Description lacks information about side effects (network removal and connection behavior change) and return value. data.current_description:
This gives "app device id", "certificate" and "signed data".
The `app_id` in the request is mandatory and refers to Chromecast backdrop/screensaver app. It has to be set to `E8C28D3C`.

The certificate is valid and issued by `Chromecast ICA 6 (Audio Assist), Google Inc`.

Not sure what the other two are.
data.description_suggestion: Generates device-specific credentials for Chromecast backdrop/screensaver applications. Requires `app_id` parameter set to `E8C28D3C`. Returns an object containing: app_device_id (unique device identifier for the app), certificate (X.509 certificate issued by Chromecast ICA 6), and signed_data (cryptographic signature). These credentials are used for secure app-device communication.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description contains uncertainty ('Not sure what the other two are') which is problematic for AI agents. data.current_description:
This gives most of the device info. The GET parameter `param` is a comma separated list of json keys to fetch. Currently, these params are known: `version,audio,name,build_info,detail,device_info,net,wifi,setup,settings,opt_in,opencast,multizone,proxy,night_mode_params,user_eq,room_equalizer,sign,aogh,ultrasound,mesh`

Nested items can also be filtered using the dot notation. Example: `audio.digital`

The `options` GET parameter is always set to `detail` or `detail,sign`. `sign` signs the `nonce` and returns some value.

The `nonce` GET parameter is an integer value signed with needed (see `option` parameter above).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves comprehensive device information and status from the Google Home device. Supports selective field retrieval via the `param` query parameter (comma-separated list of keys like version, audio, name, build_info, device_info, net, wifi, settings, etc.). Nested fields can be filtered using dot notation (e.g., `audio.digital`). The `options` parameter controls response format: use `detail` for standard info or `detail,sign` to include cryptographic signatures. The `nonce` parameter (integer) is used for signature verification when `sign` option is enabled. No authentication required for basic queries.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description is unclear about parameter usage and contains confusing language ('signed with needed'). Improved version clarifies the action, explains each parameter's purpose and valid values, provides examples, and indicates authentication requirements.

This gets and sets alarms and timers volume.

Sending an empty body gets the volume. Sending `volume` sets the volume.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves or updates the volume level for alarms and timers independently from device volume. Send an empty POST body to retrieve the current alarm volume. Send a POST body with `volume` parameter (float between 0.0 and 1.0, where 0 is minimum and 1 is maximum) to set the alarm volume. Returns the current or updated alarm volume value. Note: This setting controls only alarm and timer audio, not general device volume.

data.current_description:
*See note for Bluetooth under `/setup/bluetooth/status`*

**For Part 2 only**

This returns a list of all nearby bluetooth devices. While the Home app only shows speakers, this list contains all devices including TVs, mobiles, etc.

`rssi` is signal strength, `name` is name, `mac_address` is mac address.  
`device_class` and `device_type` are bluetooth codes.  

The Home app only lists those devices with `expected_profiles` > 0. Basically, the device should function as a speaker.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve nearby Bluetooth devices for pairing
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all nearby Bluetooth devices detected by the Google Home device (Part 2 functionality). This endpoint scans for external Bluetooth speakers and devices that can connect to Home. Returns device information including name, MAC address, signal strength (rssi), device class, device type, and expected profiles. Devices with `expected_profiles` > 0 are compatible speakers. Note: The Home app filters to show only speaker-compatible devices, but this endpoint returns all detected Bluetooth devices including phones, TVs, and other peripherals.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic and doesn't clarify the purpose (scanning for speakers to pair with). Description references external documentation and lacks clarity about the scanning context. Enhanced summary to indicate the pairing intent and improved description to explain this is for discovering external speakers/devices to connect to, clarifying the Part 2 context and making the purpose clear for AI agents.

data.current_description:
> **There are 2 parts of Bluetooth.**
>
> *Part 1*: Devices like phones connect to Home and play audio through Home.  
> For this, /setup/bluetooth/discovery is used to make Home discoverable. Then devices can connect to it as if Home is just another bluetooth speaker.
>
> *Part 2*: Bluetooth speakers connect to Home and Home plays audio through the speakers.
> For this, /setup/bluetooth/scan and /setup/bluetooth/scan_results are used to connect to other speakers.
 >
> The other endpoints are common for both parts.


**For both parts**
 
 This gives the status of all bluetooth things.
- Not sure what `audio_mode` is.
- `discovery_enabled` states whether Home is discoverable. (**Part 1**)
 - `connecting_devices` is a list of all media sources (like phones) connected to Home. (**Part 1**)
- `scanning_enabled` states whether Home scanning for other bluetooth speakers/devices. (**Part 2**)
- `connected_devices` is a list of all speakers connected to Home. (**Part 2**)
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete Bluetooth status of the Google Home device, including both inbound and outbound connections. Returns status for Part 1 (devices connecting to Home as a speaker) and Part 2 (Home connecting to external speakers). Response includes: `discovery_enabled` (whether Home is discoverable for Part 1 pairing), `connecting_devices` (list of phones/media sources connected to Home), `scanning_enabled` (whether Home is scanning for external speakers), `connected_devices` (list of external speakers connected to Home), and `audio_mode` (audio routing mode). This endpoint provides a comprehensive view of all active and available Bluetooth connections.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Status' is too generic and doesn't indicate what status is being retrieved. Description is verbose with external references and contains uncertainty ('Not sure what audio_mode is'). Enhanced summary to specify Bluetooth connection status and improved description to clearly organize the response fields by Part, remove uncertainty, and provide a comprehensive overview of what the endpoint returns for better AI agent interpretability.
